Conduct surveys on different schools covered by the City to ensure that all concerns are considered in the prioritization of projects for possible inclusion in the preparation of the SEF budget.
2. 	Review the reasonableness and necessity of the amount provided for every expense item.  Consider past transactions to come up with realistic budget, such that a bigger portion of the budget may be allocated for the construction, repair and rehabilitation of school buildings and other facilities.
3.  Consider the balances from the continuing allotments consisting of excess funds     from implemented projects to be used as source of fund for the supplemental budget.
4.  Implement projects lined up for the year funded from both the current year and continuing appropriations especially on the construction, repair and rehabilitation of school buildings and other facilities.

Recommendations:  We recommended to the City Officials and the Local School Board to:
1.	Give priority in the allocation of budget to school programs and projects that can benefit the public schools such as construction and repair of school buildings, facilities, equipment and the like by conducting surveys and inspections of all schools covered by the City especially those schools in the remote areas.
Discontinue charging to SEF expenses which do not belong to the prioritized activities of public schools to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit.

Recommendations:  We recommended that management consider the following:
1. Instruct the newly-structured GFPS and the TWG to immediately act on the recommendations of the DILG in accordance with laws, rules and regulations.
Be cautious of the timelines provided under the DBM/NEDA and the National Commission on the Role of Filipino Women Joint Circular No. 2004-1 particularly in GAD planning and budgeting stage as this is the start off point of all other activities.
2. Closely coordinate with all concerned offices/departments who were provided with budget/appropriations for GAD activities to consistently report all accomplishments as against the plans and budget to determine the efficiency of addressing gender issues and concerns of their respective sectors and constituents.
3. Identify gender issues based on the result of gender audit, gender analysis or sex disaggregated data and other references to ensure that GAD projects are responsive to agency’s problems and concerns that arise from the unequal status of women and men including the differential characteristics, roles and expectations attributed to women and men.

Recommendations: We recommended to the BAC and BAC Secretariat the following:
1. 	Immediate submission of the lacking supporting documents in every contract as listed/detailed in Annex A to facilitate the auditorial as well as technical review of contracts submitted;
2. 	To re-evaluate and re-assess the existing procurement process and be cautious in the performance of their functions and responsibilities as mandated to avoid suspensions and disallowances in audit and other possible legal impediments; and
3. 	Henceforth, see to it that complete supporting documents are attached to the contracts submitted in compliance with the provisions of the Government Procurement Reform Act (RA 9184) and its Revised IRR.

Recommendations: We recommended that:
The CCDO and CLAP shall share in the number of beneficiaries to be monitored to make it more manageable.
The LCE require the CCDO and CLAP to conduct ocular inspection of the NGOs/POs’ business site and remind them of their responsibility to pay or liquidate the funds granted to them as agreed upon in the MOA.
Require also the submission of the monthly monitoring report as basis in what course of action to take to enforce collection and/or liquidation.

Recommendations:  To address the deficiency, we recommended that the City:
1. Create a committee to conduct the complete physical count of all property of the City as of December 31, 2016.
As previously recommended in prior years’ AAR, to facilitate the early completion of the physical count, coordinate with all the Department Heads to provide the Committee with the list of PPEs in their department for their validation.
2	The Office of the City General Officer shall prepare the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment which shall be reconciled with the accounting records; copy furnished the Auditor’s Office.
3.	Identify the unserviceable properties. Prepare the pertinent supporting documents then request for inspection and disposal.

Recommendations: To address the deficiency, we recommended that the City Government:
1.	Prioritize the completion of the program on how to generate the monthly depreciation based on the reduced residual value of 5%; and
2.	Once established, prepare the appropriate adjusting entry so that the PPE shall be fairly presented.

Recommendation: We recommended to require the concerned responsible and liable persons to settle the suspended and disallowed amounts totaling P19,529,739.41 and P1,786,218.56, respectively pursuant to Sections 9.4 and 10.4, Chapter III of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
